summary: Non-coding variations in or near MEF2C—such as regulatory elements or repeat expansions—can disrupt gene function and lead to developmental disorders independently of coding sequence alterations. Evidence from similar genetic conditions demonstrates that both coding and non-coding mutations contribute to disease pathology; thus, it is incorrect to claim that only coding changes cause these disorders.
formatted_summary: agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Non-coding variations in or near MEF2C—such as regulatory elements or repeat expansions—can disrupt gene function and lead to developmental disorders independently of coding sequence alterations. Evidence from similar genetic conditions demonstrates that both coding and non-coding mutations contribute to disease pathology; thus, it is incorrect to claim that only coding changes cause these disorders.. Answer choice: B
